sanskrit:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते।
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात्॥
transliteration: nehābhikrama-nāśo ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyate | svalpam apy asya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt ||
literal_meaning: In this effort there is no loss or diminution, and even a little of this dharma saves from great fear.
contextual_meaning: Any step toward self-transcendent action is never wasted; micro-gains are cumulative and protect against existential dread.
psychological_state: Anxious perfectionist reassured that partial efforts count and cannot be lost.
trigger_scenario: A student afraid to start meditation because she can't sit for 20 minutes is told that even 30 seconds of mindful breathing is banked forever.
anchor_text: Every selfless step, however small, is permanent progress against fear.
